solutions.solution:
Refer to sample passage 5.
Inference: I All the scientific discoveries have been made by old scientists.
II due to success in scientific research, freshness of outlook is not necessary.
III Maturity due to age and experience is not an important requirement for success in scientific research.
Explanation: The first inference says that all the scientific discoveries have been made by old scientists. This is exactly opposite of a statement made in the passage that majority of discoveries have been made by the youth. Hence, the inference is definitely false. The second inference is a restatement of the second sentence of the passage. Hence, it is definitely true. The same sentence goes on to say that the "most important requirement" for success in scientific research is not the maturity of knowledge associated with age and experience. The third inference is more or less a restatement of the same. Hence, it is definitely true.
